introSection:
Wild Mouse was a hybrid wild mouse roller coaster built by Butlin's (under license from B. A. Schiff & Associates) previously located at Botton's Pleasure Beach in Skegness, Lincolnshire, England, UK. It opened for the 1962 season, replacing the park's Big Dipper. Signs outside the roller coaster referred to it as both Mad Mouse and Wild Mouse.

A photograph dated June 6, 1971 contains this roller coaster. The spot it stood at was previously occupied by the Scenic Railway. It was replaced by the Zyklon and was located in the same place that the Rockin Roller is now.
fullText:
Wild Mouse was a hybrid wild mouse roller coaster built by Butlin's (under license from B. A. Schiff & Associates) previously located at Botton's Pleasure Beach in Skegness, Lincolnshire, England, UK. It opened for the 1962 season, replacing the park's Big Dipper. Signs outside the roller coaster referred to it as both Mad Mouse and Wild Mouse.

A photograph dated June 6, 1971 contains this roller coaster. The spot it stood at was previously occupied by the Scenic Railway. It was replaced by the Zyklon and was located in the same place that the Rockin Roller is now.
